title: undefined reference to `VlThreadPool::VlThreadPool error when compiling
body:
OS: Pop!_OS 22.04 LTS
Pyenv: 3.11.1
GCC: gcc (Ubuntu 11.3.0-1ubuntu1~22.04.1) 11.3.0
Kernel: 6.2.6

I'm getting this error when compiling :

`g++ -I /home/fortyone/Documents/verilator/include -I /home/fortyone/Documents/verilator/include/vltstd main.o Vtoplevel___024root__DepSet_h887c3000__0.o Vtoplevel___024root__DepSet_h887c3000__0__Slow.o Vtoplevel___024root__DepSet_h9890b585__0.o Vtoplevel___024root__DepSet_h9890b585__0__Slow.o Vtoplevel___024root__Slow.o Vtoplevel___024unit__DepSet_h1dabdd97__0__Slow.o Vtoplevel___024unit__Slow.o Vtoplevel__ConstPool_0.o Vtoplevel.o Vtoplevel__Syms.o /home/fortyone/Documents/verilator/include/verilated.cpp -o testbench`

The error I get:

```
/usr/bin/ld: /tmp/ccRF5bL0.o: in function `VerilatedContext::threadPoolp()':
verilated.cpp:(.text+0xdc28): undefined reference to `VlThreadPool::VlThreadPool(VerilatedContext*, unsigned int)'
```

verilator --version 

```
$ verilator --version
Verilator 5.010 2023-04-30 rev v5.010-33-gc37ec1975
```

I'm still learning to use Verilator. Help is appreciated. Thanks !
code_blocks:
/usr/bin/ld: /tmp/ccRF5bL0.o: in function `VerilatedContext::threadPoolp()':
verilated.cpp:(.text+0xdc28): undefined reference to `VlThreadPool::VlThreadPool(VerilatedContext*, unsigned int)'
